population: Humans represented in periodization meta-analyses; underlying studies were strength training
intervention: Systematic review of periodized versus non-periodized exercise meta-analyses
comparator: Varied non-periodized comparators and prediction-accuracy tests
outcome_measure: Presence of varied comparator; prospective accuracy of predicted timing
missingness: Running population, youth strata, dose, and timing details NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: None of the audited studies tested varied non-periodized comparators or predicted adaptation timing
uncertainty: No distance-running evidence; no 7-day versus 9/9.5/10-day comparison
limitations: Strength-only evidence; does not validate any nonweekly running frame or predict recovery/adaptation completion

population: 106 coaches; 58% individual-sport coaches; 32% coached international-level athletes
intervention: Convenience online survey of planning concepts
outcome_measure: Agreement with fixed adaptation timeframes and fixed targets
missingness: Running-specific subgroup and behavior verification NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 33% agreed adaptations are achievable within fixed timeframes; 76% disagreed targets should remain fixed
uncertainty: Self-selection; perceptions do not establish efficacy or cycle prevalence
limitations: No running-specific subgroup; 92% male; no 9/9.5/10-day question or outcome efficacy

population: 12 successful male Norwegian coaches across eight Olympic endurance sports
intervention: Questionnaire, training-log cross-reference, and interviews describing endurance planning
component_dose: 2-3 key workout days and 3-5 intensive sessions per week across sports
component_order: Weekly organization with pragmatic adjustments for travel, camps, and competition
outcome_measure: Reported weekly load, key-workout frequency, intensive-session frequency
instrument_protocol: FULL_TEXT_VERIFIED; Crossmark current on audit date
missingness: Athlete-level outcomes and exact runner subgroup details NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Weekly organization remained explicit; adjustments were pragmatic
uncertainty: Descriptive and partly retrospective; no causal comparison
limitations: All-male coaches; middle-distance under six minutes excluded; no exact nonweekly-cycle question; cannot establish standard 9.5-day practice

intervention: Narrative integration of scientific literature and results-proven practice
component_dose: 11-14 sessions per week; typical training weeks
outcome_measure: Sessions per week and typical-week structures
missingness: Sex-stratified details and exact source-level schedules NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Successful practice described in weekly units
uncertainty: Narrative selected-practice synthesis; no causal frame comparison
limitations: Long-distance adults rather than youth 800/1500; no nonweekly comparison; weekly reporting may reflect source convention

intervention: Coach-prescribed near-sea-level then live-high train-high training
comparator: Within-athlete context across near-sea-level and altitude; no frame comparator
component_dose: 4 weeks near sea level and 3-4 weeks at 2100 m
component_order: Typical training week with athlete-specific changes by event, physiology, and altitude experience
outcome_measure: Weekly training characteristics and athlete-specific adjustments
missingness: Adolescent stratum and nonweekly schedule NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Typical weekly organization coexisted with individual adjustment
uncertainty: n=8 observational camp case; no comparator
individual_response: Athlete-specific event and altitude changes reported
limitations: Adult altitude-camp context; no 9/9.5/10-day frame; does not authorize efficacy or safety claims

intervention: Approximately 9-9.5 consecutive days of supervised twice-daily intervals plus glutamine or placebo
component_dose: Twice-daily intervals for approximately 9-9.5 consecutive days; five recovery days
outcome_measure: Glutamine, nasal and salivary IgA, withdrawal, compliance
missingness: Performance, coaching use, and recurring-cycle outcomes NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Primary supplement outcomes; 96% compliance
uncertainty: Very small sample; no seven-day comparator
limitations: False-positive exact duration: consecutive overload exposure, not recurring microcycle; prohibited as evidence for 9.5-day Formation efficacy or safety

population: Meb Keflezighi, elite adult male marathoner
intervention: Retrospective athlete-described 9-day training cycle
component_dose: One tempo run, one interval session, and one long run per 9-day cycle; other running adjusted by feel
component_order: TEMPO;INTERVAL;LONG_RUN across 9 days; exact dates NOT_REPORTED
outcome_measure: Named use of cycle; Boston win; 2:08:37 personal best context
missingness: Exact daily dose, actual intervals, adherence, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Verified self-report of actual 9-day use; performance is context only
uncertainty: Single retrospective case; no causal attribution
limitations: Adult marathoner with injury-history context; no youth/middle-distance transfer and no exact 9.5-day use

intervention: Self-programmed 9-day cycle
component_dose: Threshold/easy/easy repeated; third quality exposure was a long run
separation_hours: Approximately 48 h between quality exposures by pattern; exact hours NOT_VERIFIED
outcome_measure: Named use; reported 2:56 personal best; recovery and enjoyment
missingness: Exact age, full dose, actual intervals, and confounders NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Self-reported personal best and better recovery/enjoyment
uncertainty: Single self-programmed case; concurrent training changes; weekday feasibility issues
limitations: Does not prove performance benefit, recovery completion, youth suitability, prevalence, or 9.5-day optimality/safety

population: Adult distance runners and coaches including Lindsey Scherf and Thom Hunt
intervention: Coach/athlete interviews plus 9-day, 10-day, and 14-day practice templates
component_dose: Template doses vary; Thom Hunt reported actual 10-day use
separation_hours: A quoted 72 h preference; not physiological clearance
outcome_measure: Named 10-day use; template existence; maintenance and schedule conflicts
missingness: Measured outcomes, denominator, and actual interval histories NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Verified self-report of 10-day use and verified 9-day template; nonweekly schedules sometimes abandoned
uncertainty: Anecdotes and templates; no systematic sample or outcome testing
individual_response: Reports difficulty maintaining 10-day routine and disruption of partners/social schedules
limitations: 72 h is a stated preference only; cannot establish clearance, universal recovery, efficacy, safety, or prevalence

population: Distance runners; named Hansons Olympic Development Program and Ray Treacy athletes
intervention: Coaching article reporting 10-day to two-week cycles
component_dose: Hansons reportedly 10 days; Treacy reportedly 10 days to two weeks
outcome_measure: Third-party report of program cycle length and perceived scheduling/recovery benefits
missingness: Athlete denominator, exact protocol, outcomes, and primary confirmation NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Reported 10-day use only; not primary verified
uncertainty: Third-party coaching article; unsupported benefit and injury language
limitations: Cannot support prevalence, efficacy, injury prevention, safety, or exact 9.5-day mechanics

intervention: Coach-authored 9-day sample plan and 10-day extension advice
component_dose: Long run; two quality sessions; easy/rest; strength/cross-training; recovery across 9 days
component_order: Template order reported on source page; actual-use order NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Existence of 9-day sample and 10-day mention
missingness: Implementation, adherence, outcomes, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Verified plan only; actual use NOT_FOUND_IN_SOURCE
uncertainty: Advice/template without measured cohort
limitations: Cannot substantiate common use, optimality, safety, recovery completion, or TRAINORACLE numerical policy

population: Sprint and hurdle athletes at high-school, college, and professional levels
intervention: Official coaching-symposium 10-day plan
component_dose: Speed, tempo, regeneration, strength, off, and flex days
outcome_measure: Presented 10-day setup and meet-schedule rationale
missingness: Athlete sample, adherence, outcomes, exact intervals, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Verified plan only; actual use NOT_VERIFIED
uncertainty: Wrong-event directness; presentation without outcomes
limitations: Competition anchors may inform mechanics, but sprint programming cannot determine youth middle-distance dose, interval, efficacy, or safety

population: Emma Bates, elite adult female marathoner
intervention: Athlete-described 10-day cycle
component_dose: Two workouts and a long run; three recovery days after long run
component_order: Exact workout order beyond reported components NOT_REPORTED
separation_hours: Three days after long run; exact hours NOT_VERIFIED
outcome_measure: Verified 10-day use; fifth place; 2:22:10 context
missingness: Exact daily dose, actual hours, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Verified athlete self-report of 10-day use; performance is context only
uncertainty: Single athlete; no comparator or causal attribution
limitations: Corrects conflicting 9-day overview; no evidence for 9 or 9.5 days; no youth/middle-distance transfer or safety inference

intervention: All-out 21-km run
outcome_measure: TBARS; uric acid; glutathione; XO; SOD; CAT
missingness: Performance, force, gait, subjective recovery, and maturity NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: CAT and TBARS elevated at 24 h versus pre; other domains not measured
uncertainty: Small male-only sample; abstract-level estimates
limitations: Extreme endurance bout; no comparator; residual biomarker is not harm, readiness, safety, or clearance

intervention: All-out 21-km run
outcome_measure: Urea; creatinine; CK; LDH; Hb/Hct; calculated eGFR; plasma-volume change
missingness: Performance, force, gait, maturity, and hydration detail NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Early renal and muscle-marker changes reported sustained at 24 h
uncertainty: Small male-only cohort; hydration-sensitive calculations; abstract-level detail
limitations: Extreme event; biomarkers cannot be converted into diagnosis, safety judgment, readiness, or clearance

population: 12 trained male adolescent multi-sport endurance athletes
intervention: One-day 48.2-km and two-day 66.0-km adventure races 10 weeks apart
comparator: Pre-race baseline and within-athlete alternate race format
component_dose: Trail running; cycling; kayaking; skating across 48.2 or 66.0 km
outcome_measure: Drop jump; squat jump; MVIC; soreness; cTnI; CK-MB
missingness: No female athletes; no cardiac-function measure; no blood after day 1 of two-day race
effect_estimate: Drop-jump height -5.9% post; squat jump and MVIC unchanged; soreness elevated at 24 h and not different at 72 h; most cardiac markers toward baseline 24-48 h except CK-MB after two-day race
uncertainty: n=12; mixed modalities and durations; marker-specific time points
limitations: No whole-athlete recovery or safety inference; target interval 72 h is comparison context only, never clearance

intervention: 90-minute match with no subsequent training
outcome_measure: Isometric posterior-chain peak force
missingness: Cohort-specific day-to-day reliability NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Force -18% post, -8% at 24 h, not different at 48 h, +12% at 72 h
uncertainty: One team and one match; familiarization may affect 72-h value
limitations: Soccer indirectness; a single force test and elapsed 72 h do not establish clearance, readiness, or safety

population: 10 male elite youth academy soccer players
intervention: Competitive match followed by static stretching or passive recovery crossover
comparator: Static stretching versus passive recovery; pre-match baseline
outcome_measure: CMJ; soreness; CK
missingness: No 24-h or 72-h measure
effect_estimate: CMJ remained lower and soreness/CK elevated at 48 h in both conditions; stretching differed only for CK at 48 h
uncertainty: n=10; intervention confounds natural kinetics
limitations: Soccer-specific; cannot set runner interval; no clearance or safety inference

intervention: Official match
outcome_measure: Low-frequency fatigue; CMJ; fatigue; recovery; soreness
missingness: Biochemical, gait, 72-h, and full protocol details NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Low-frequency fatigue reduced post but not different at 24 h; CMJ unchanged; subjective and soreness measures impaired through 48 h
uncertainty: Abstract-only; male soccer
individual_response: Objective and subjective marker domains disagreed
limitations: No universal recovery state; 72 h was not observed and remains a target only, not clearance

population: 48 Italian Serie A youth soccer players
intervention: Friendly match then randomized complete or reduced training at 48 h
comparator: Complete training n=26 versus reduced training n=22; pre-match baseline
separation_hours: 48 h between match and manipulated training
outcome_measure: Posterior-chain force; CMJ; soreness; fatigue
missingness: No 24-h point; no biochemical outcome
effect_estimate: At 48 h force and soreness altered while CMJ and subjective fatigue were not; complete training reduced CMJ/force from 48 to 72 h versus reduced training
uncertainty: Friendly match; 72-h state includes intervening load
individual_response: Domains diverged and assigned load altered 72-h state
limitations: Strong counterevidence to elapsed-time-only inference; cannot isolate match as sole cause or treat 72 h as clearance

population: 32 male U17/U19 elite academy soccer players with limited structured-strength experience
intervention: Fixed-order crossover: back squats plus calf raises versus back squats plus hurdle/drop jumps
component_dose: Shared squat stimulus; exact set/repetition detail in full text
outcome_measure: CMJ; drop jump; IMTP; adductor strength; self-report; CK
missingness: Sleep, nutrition, sprint, central-fatigue, chronic, and adverse-event endpoints NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: No modality-by-time interaction; largest fatigue 24-48 h; CK peaked 48 h; selected markers near baseline by 72 h
uncertainty: Non-random order; unfamiliar strength work; shared squat stimulus
limitations: Near-baseline selected markers do not establish safety, whole-athlete recovery, or next-MAIN clearance

population: Youth and adult participants in exercise-induced muscle-damage studies
intervention: Systematic review/meta-analysis of youth-adult comparisons without recovery treatment
component_dose: Studies reported strength, soreness, or CK at >=24 h
outcome_measure: Peak strength loss; soreness; CK
missingness: Session-specific kinetics and runner strata NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Peak effects larger in adults; limb meta-regression for soreness but not strength/CK
uncertainty: Peak effect is not time to normalization; pooled heterogeneous protocols
limitations: Indirect review cannot clear an individual adolescent or define a universal interval; 72 h never clearance

intervention: Two self-paced treadmill 1500-m time trials
comparator: Trial 1 versus trial 2; prior measurement error
outcome_measure: 1500-m time/speed; step variables; physiology; exertion
missingness: No tactical race; other recovery domains incomplete
effect_estimate: Time/speed, step variables, and most physiology differed no more than measurement error; higher exertion about 2.5 times more likely on trial 2
uncertainty: n=12 adults; treadmill; early season; carbon shoes; considerable uncertainty
limitations: Preserved 1500-m performance at 24 h does not mean other outcomes normalized or repeated MAIN is safe/cleared

intervention: 60-minute downhill run at -10% and 65% maximal HR
outcome_measure: Vertical ground-reaction-force peaks; CK; MRI T2; pain
missingness: Exact pain result and 72-h state NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Selected vGRF peaks reduced through 24-48 h; CK elevated 24 and 48 h; anterior-thigh MRI T2 elevated to 48 h
uncertainty: Abstract-only; contrived eccentric-damage session; asymmetric findings
limitations: Male recreational adults; no universal interval, diagnosis, safety, readiness, or clearance inference

intervention: Two 30-minute downhill bouts at -20% and 2.8 m/s, three weeks apart
outcome_measure: MVC; peripheral indices; gait; soreness; CK
missingness: CK time point internally inconsistent between methods and results
effect_estimate: Bout 1 MVC lower through 48 h; gait altered up to 72 h; soreness elevated through 96 h and not different at 168 h; bout 2 responses smaller/shorter
uncertainty: n=10 men; CK subset n=6; fixed speed; treadmill; strong repeated-bout effect
limitations: Explicit >72-h soreness counterevidence does not define a new universal interval; 72 h never clearance

intervention: Recumbent-cycle repeated-sprint sessions separated by randomized 24 or 48 h
outcome_measure: Sprint output; chair MVC; Db100
missingness: Youth, running, soreness, biochemical, and full protocol details NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Sprint output did not differ; at 24 h MVC -6.5% and Db100 -10.4%; deficits not reported at 48 h
uncertainty: n=10; cycling; abstract-only; repeated-session design
individual_response: Performance and neuromuscular domains disagreed at 24 h
limitations: Preserved output cannot erase concurrent neuromuscular difference or establish recovery/clearance

population: Healthy adults and adolescents across 20 studies
intervention: Systematic review/meta-analysis of muscle-damaging resistance or plyometric protocols
component_dose: Heterogeneous protocols requiring sprint/COD follow-up >=24 h
outcome_measure: Sprint; COD; CMJ; DOMS; CK
missingness: Youth middle-distance stratum and many controls NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Pooled sprint/COD/CMJ impairments and DOMS/CK elevations reported up to 72 h
uncertainty: CK/DOMS I2 65-81%; varied histories/protocols; English-only; overlap pending
limitations: Cannot set a youth runner threshold or treat 72 h as recovery, readiness, safety, or permission

intervention: Systematic review of post-match recovery with at least two measures
comparator: Pre-match baseline and study-specific repeated measures
outcome_measure: Sprint/performance; CK; cortisol
missingness: No direct youth-runner stratum; source-level overlap not mapped
effect_estimate: Performance may return before biochemical markers; CK time courses to >=72 h; soccer/rugby tended longer
uncertainty: Heterogeneous sports/protocols; abstract-level extraction
limitations: No direct 800/1500 youth inference; performance normalization cannot establish whole-athlete recovery or clearance

population: 25 postpubertal adolescent distance runners enrolled; 18 completed
intervention: Twice-weekly plyometric, sprint, and resistance work added to normal running
comparator: Strength intervention n=9 completers versus control n=9 completers
component_dose: 10 weeks; secondary table reports 2-4 sets, 6-15 repetitions, 90-180 s rest
outcome_measure: Economy; sprint; MVC; aerobic and body-composition measures
missingness: Maturity method, exact sequence, running separation, and attrition reasons NOT_VERIFIED
effect_estimate: Economy about 3.2-3.7% possibly beneficial; sprint effect .32; MVC effect .86; aerobic/body-composition trivial/small
uncertainty: Small completer sample; magnitude-based descriptions; seven of 25 did not complete
limitations: Supports component inclusion only; cannot define order, interval, whole 9.5-day efficacy, safety, or clearance

intervention: Concurrent endurance plus explosive training replacing part of endurance volume
comparator: Concurrent n=13 versus endurance control n=12
component_dose: 8 weeks; 19% of endurance replaced; secondary review reports one explosive session/week, 2-3 sets x 6-10 reps; about 7.2 versus 8.5 endurance h/week
outcome_measure: Maximal anaerobic running speed; 30-m speed; aerobic maximal speed; VO2max; economy
missingness: Sex, maturity, exact day/order/separation NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Maximal anaerobic running speed +3.0 +/- 2.0%; 30-m speed +1.1 +/- 1.3%; VO2max/economy unchanged
uncertainty: Small groups; abstract-level scheduling detail
limitations: Component substitution evidence only; not superiority of sequence or 9.5-day architecture

population: 12 competitive youth runners pair-matched by maturity and 3-km performance
intervention: Continuous and interval running plus machine resistance
component_dose: 10 weeks; running twice weekly; resistance reported twice weekly in abstract but once weekly at 3-4 x 10-12 and 70-75% 1RM in review table
outcome_measure: 3-km time
instrument_protocol: ABSTRACT_ONLY; full-text located but not extracted
missingness: Resistance frequency conflict; sequence and separation NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: No significant interaction/main effect; post-test difference 38 s adverse to concurrent; review reports +6 s versus -17 s
uncertainty: Extremely small groups and unresolved dose reporting
limitations: Possible interference signal, not proof; cannot define scheduling, safety, or 9.5-day mechanics

population: 17 national or international male junior middle-distance runners
intervention: Depth jumps before submaximal run and time-to-exhaustion test
comparator: Body-weight quarter-squat control in randomized crossover
component_dose: 5-min warm-up; 5-min economy run; six depth jumps; 10-min passive recovery; 5-min run; time to exhaustion at vVO2max
outcome_measure: Economy; time to exhaustion; other physiological measures
instrument_protocol: ABSTRACT_ONLY; author PDF located but not fully extracted
missingness: Chronic placement and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Economy improved 3.7%; effect-size 95% CI 0.25-1.09; time to exhaustion and other physiology trivial
uncertainty: Acute potentiation; abstract-level full protocol confidence
limitations: Narrow acute sequence only; cannot establish chronic same-day/adjacent-day scheduling or universal order

intervention: Complex training versus heavy resistance versus low-load control alongside endurance
comparator: Matched complex, heavy-resistance, and control groups
component_dose: 8 weeks; 9 sessions/week; 6 endurance and 3 strength; complex pairs used 3 x 5 at 80-85% 1RM then 3 x 6 plyometrics with 4-min recovery
component_order: Morning endurance then afternoon strength; within strength heavy resistance before matched plyometric
separation_hours: AM-PM exact hours NOT_REPORTED; 4 min within complex pairs
outcome_measure: 1RM; CMJ; drop jump; RSI; economy; 50-m; 5-km; lactate; VO2max
missingness: Exact AM-PM hours and reverse-order condition NOT_REPORTED
adverse_events: Injury-free eligibility; intervention adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Complex and heavy groups improved several outcomes; complex-only gains in drop jump, RSI, economy at 16 km/h, and lactate; VO2max unchanged
uncertainty: Small matched groups; allocation not clearly randomized
limitations: Supports narrow heavy-to-plyometric pair only; no youth threshold, reverse-order result, safety, or whole-frame validation

intervention: Same-session speed endurance followed by heavy resistance with reduced running volume
comparator: Intervention n=12 versus control n=11; no reverse-order comparator
component_dose: 8 weeks; twice weekly 30-s speed-endurance reps with 3-min recovery then resistance at 80-90% 1RM; other weekly runs; running volume reduced 42%
separation_hours: Same session; about 3-min recoveries within components
outcome_measure: Race/time tests; economy; strength; NHE1; VO2max; muscle morphology/capillarization
missingness: No reverse order or separation comparison; adverse events NOT_REPORTED
adverse_events: Consent mentioned risks/discomfort; intervention events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 10 km improved by week 4; 1500 m, Yo-Yo, economy, strength, and NHE1 improved by week 8; VO2max/morphology/capillarization unchanged
uncertainty: Adult male sample; concurrent adherence 76 +/- 4%; substitution confounding
limitations: Direct adult same-session example only; cannot establish universal order, youth rule, safety, or 9.5-day superiority

population: 20 well-trained runners randomized across five uphill programs
intervention: Five uphill interval-training programs
separation_hours: Placement relative to other work NOT_REPORTED
outcome_measure: 5-km time; economy; neuromuscular and aerobic measures
missingness: Age, sex, exact dose, placement, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Across programs 5-km improvement about 2.0% +/- 0.6%; highest intensity favored economy about 2.4% +/- 1.4%; no clearly superior program
uncertainty: About four per condition; model-based comparisons
limitations: Hill-component evidence only; no composite placement or universal fatigue/order inference

intervention: Run HIIT versus cycle HIIT while habitual training continued
comparator: Run HIIT n=7 versus cycle HIIT n=7
component_dose: Twice weekly for 4 weeks; 10-min warm-up; 6 x 2 min at 95% HRmax with 2-min active recovery; 4 x 1 min all-out with 90-s recovery; 5-min cool-down
separation_hours: Placement relative to habitual training NOT_REPORTED
outcome_measure: Treadmill VO2max; 10-km time; cycle pace; RPE; soreness; CK
missingness: Habitual-training placement not standardized/reported
effect_estimate: Run-HIIT VO2max 42.1 to 45.2; cycle-HIIT no comparable rise; 10-km unchanged; cycling pace small change; cycling RPE/soreness increased
uncertainty: Tiny older all-female sample
limitations: Cycling is not established as equivalent, lower fatigue, or recovery; highly indirect for adolescents

population: Seven heterogeneous randomized trials from untrained through competitive adults
intervention: Systematic review/meta-analysis of running-only, cycling-only, and combined protocols
comparator: Running versus cycling or combined running/cycling
component_dose: At least 4 weeks; protocols heterogeneous; trials dated 1974-2003
outcome_measure: Standardized effects for VO2max and performance
missingness: Source-level order, placement, adverse events, and several participant fields NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Treadmill VO2max g=-.32 [-.76,.13]; cycle VO2max g=-.34 [-.79,.11]; running performance g=.02 [-.62,.66]; all null
uncertainty: Wide intervals; old heterogeneous trials; substantial risk-of-bias concerns
limitations: Null results do not establish modality equivalence, recovery benefit, or placement rule

intervention: Concurrent aerobic plus resistance versus identical resistance-only training
component_dose: At least 4 weeks; running in 16 studies; cycling in 24
separation_hours: Same-session <=20 min versus separated >=3 h study categories
outcome_measure: Meta-analytic SMDs
missingness: Source-level sequence and adverse events not fully recoverable
effect_estimate: Max strength SMD -.06 [-.20,.09]; hypertrophy -.01 [-.16,.18]; explosive strength -.28 [-.48,-.08]; same-session explosive subgroup -.31 [-.62,-.01]
uncertainty: Mean PEDro 4.3/10; observational between-study subgroup patterns; heterogeneous adults
limitations: Study-category gaps are not experimentally established youth thresholds; no universal same-session rule

intervention: Same-session resistance-before-endurance versus endurance-before-resistance
component_dose: Interventions at least 5 weeks; source-level doses heterogeneous
outcome_measure: Meta-analytic weighted mean differences
missingness: Power only two studies; source-level adverse events and dose incomplete
effect_estimate: Resistance-first dynamic strength +6.91% [1.96,11.87]; no order effect for hypertrophy, static strength, or aerobic capacity
uncertainty: I2 66% for dynamic strength; adult non-runner specificity
limitations: Does not justify universal strength-first; detectable effect was outcome-specific and not a youth middle-distance rule

intervention: Concurrent resistance then aerobic training at 0, 6, or 24 h separation
comparator: 0 h versus 6 h versus 24 h versus strength-only versus control
component_dose: 7 weeks; two sessions of each quality per week
outcome_measure: Strength gains; VO2peak
missingness: Complete effect estimates/CIs and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Strength gains lower at 0 h than 6 h, 24 h, or strength-only; VO2peak improved in all concurrent groups and described larger at 24 h
uncertainty: Magnitude-based inference; single indirect team-sport trial
limitations: Cannot establish universal 6-h or 24-h threshold, especially for adolescents; no safety/clearance inference

population: 18 healthy inactive adults after two weeks unilateral knee immobilization
intervention: Immediate endurance-to-resistance versus resistance-to-endurance rehabilitation training
component_dose: 6 weeks; 3 sessions/week; at least one rest day; 22.5-min cycling at 65-75% VO2peak plus 3 x 10 leg exercises at 65-80% 1RM; about 45 min total
outcome_measure: Complex II activity; strength; mass; VO2peak; acute expression
missingness: Training adverse events NOT_REPORTED
adverse_events: Immobilization pain/edema monitored; training events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Order affected complex II activity but not strength, mass, or VO2peak; gene-expression interactions null p=.20-.87
uncertainty: Strong rehabilitation and detraining indirectness; small sample
limitations: Null rehab result should not override trained-runner evidence or create universal order/safety rule

intervention: Meta-analysis of concurrent aerobic and resistance training
comparator: Concurrent versus resistance-only or study-specific comparator
component_dose: Endurance modality, frequency, and duration examined as moderators
component_order: Source-level order not recoverable from abstract
separation_hours: Source-level separation not recoverable from abstract
outcome_measure: Meta-analytic interference outcomes and moderators
missingness: Exact source-level scheduling and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Historical interference signal, particularly power; effects varied with endurance mode/dose
uncertainty: Older synthesis with substantial overlap; weighted below updated review
limitations: No youth-runner calendar rule; cannot support universal sequence, fatigue number, 9.5-day efficacy, or safety

intervention: Twelve weeks of block-periodized endurance training
outcome_measure: Selected physiological and performance indices
missingness: Age, sex, exact dose, adverse events, and source-level intervals NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Small n=15 study favored block periodization on selected indices
uncertainty: Abstract-limited; small adult cycling sample
limitations: Indirect component-periodization evidence only; no running, youth, exact 9.5-day frame, recovery, safety, or clearance claim

intervention: Load-matched increasing, decreasing, or mixed high-intensity periodization
outcome_measure: Study-reported endurance adaptation outcomes
missingness: Sample size, sex, exact intervals, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED_IN_LEDGER
effect_estimate: No between-group adaptation difference reported
uncertainty: Abstract-limited adult cycling transfer
limitations: Counters a universal best ordering model; does not select 9.5 days or imply recovery/safety

population: One world-class adult female cross-country skier
intervention: Descriptive history of block versus traditional periodization
component_dose: Successful 7-11-day HIT blocks; exact session doses source-specific
outcome_measure: Training organization and career performance context
missingness: Causal counterfactual, youth transfer, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Describes successful 7-11-day HIT blocks in one athlete
uncertainty: n=1 retrospective history; no causal attribution
limitations: Not running or 9.5-day whole-frame evidence; cannot establish efficacy, safety, prevalence, or clearance

population: Trained and well-trained adult-dominant endurance athletes across mixed sports
intervention: Systematic review/meta-analysis of block periodization
outcome_measure: Pooled physiological and performance effects
missingness: Youth 800/1500 strata, exact frame intervals, and safety outcomes incomplete
effect_estimate: Small favorable pooled VO2max and maximal-power effects
uncertainty: Small low-quality heterogeneous studies; mean PEDro about 3.7/10
limitations: Duplicate bibliographic identity with FRV2 A4 source family; no 7-day versus 9/9.5/10-day comparison or superiority inference

intervention: Review contrasting event demands and training practices
component_dose: Source-specific training volumes and intensity distributions
outcome_measure: Differences between 800-m and 1500-m training characteristics
missingness: Adolescent strata, exact cycle length, and safety outcomes NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Supports retaining event-specific differences
uncertainty: Review/practice synthesis; no direct calendar-frame comparison
limitations: Supports separating 800 and 1500 inputs only; no adolescent 9.5-day efficacy or safety conclusion

intervention: Two seven-day taper protocols
comparator: Lower-volume/higher-intensity taper versus alternate seven-day taper
outcome_measure: Time-trial/performance and study-reported taper indices
missingness: Age, sex, exact dose, washout, and adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Lower volume with retained or increased intensity favored selected outcomes in this tiny crossover

population: Highly trained and elite distance runners across 10 articles

population: 12 exercise participants and 12 resting controls; participant details incompletely reported

population: 10 competitive sprinters, long jumpers, and soccer players

outcome_measure: CK; DOMS; eccentric torque; 30-m sprint; CMJ

population: 73 U19/U23 soccer players; match participants and non-participants
